articles.title: A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
articles.summary: Folk musician Murphy Campbell discovered AI-generated covers of her songs uploaded to Spotify without her permission. The incident highlights vulnerabilities in copyright enforcement and the misuse of AI voice cloning technology.

articles.title: Really, you made this without AI? Prove it
articles.summary: As AI-generated content becomes harder to distinguish from human-made work, platforms struggle with labeling. The article suggests implementing universal labels for human-created content, similar to Fair Trade certification.

articles.title: Anthropic essentially bans OpenClaw from Claude by making subscribers pay extra
articles.summary: Anthropic requires separate pay-as-you-go billing for OpenClaw users starting April 4th, effectively making the third-party tool more expensive and less accessible through regular Claude subscriptions.

articles.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
articles.summary: Anthropic will charge Claude Code subscribers additional fees to use OpenClaw and other third-party tools with their coding assistant, increasing overall costs.

articles.title: Anthropic is having a moment in the private markets; SpaceX could spoil the party
articles.summary: Anthropic dominates private market trading as the hottest stock, while OpenAI loses momentum. SpaceX's upcoming IPO could reshape the entire private investment landscape.

articles.title: OpenAI executive shuffle includes new role for COO Brad Lightcap to lead ‘special projects’
articles.summary: OpenAI COO Brad Lightcap transitions to lead special projects. CMO Kate Rouch departs temporarily for health recovery, planning future return.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, secured $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The investment capitalizes on developer demand for simpler, cost-effective alternatives to legacy cloud platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-200 monthly pricing has frustrated developers, spurring interest in Goose, a free open-source alternative by Block offering similar AI coding capabilities without subscription costs.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge. The creative recruitment stunt successfully attracted top engineering talent in a competitive market.

articles.title: OpenClaw gives users yet another reason to be freaked out about security
articles.summary: OpenClaw, a popular AI agent tool with 347,000 GitHub stars, poses security risks by requiring broad system access to perform tasks. A recently fixed vulnerability highlights dangers of granting such extensive permissions to autonomous tools.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: Researchers discovered three new Rowhammer attacks enabling malicious users to gain root control of machines running Nvidia GPUs in shared cloud environments. The attacks exploit memory bit flips caused by repeated rapid memory access.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: New research shows quantum computers could break elliptic curve encryption with far fewer resources than previously thought, with one approach cracking 256-bit ECC in 10 days using 100 times less overhead.

articles.title: Four things we’d need to put data centers in space
articles.summary: SpaceX proposes launching one million orbital data centers to support AI growth without environmental impact on Earth. Major tech companies like Amazon and Google are also exploring space-based computing infrastructure.

articles.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from the Iran conflict threaten plastic supply chains and costs. Petrochemical-dependent plastic production faces economic ripple effects affecting American consumers.

articles.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from Middle East tensions are driving up gasoline costs and threatening plastic supply chains. Petrochemical-dependent plastic production may face significant economic impacts as oil bottlenecks persist.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, a frontier multimodal AI model designed to run efficiently on-device. The model advances local AI capabilities for processing text, images, and other data types.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer interaction technology, advancing how users engage with digital systems. The innovation pushes boundaries in human-computer interfaces and interaction methods.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Falcon Perception advances AI visual recognition capabilities. The system demonstrates improved accuracy in detecting and analyzing complex scenes through enhanced perception algorithms.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of artificial intelligence, exploring emerging trends and developments in the AI field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to boost productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released an open-source toolkit for future farming technology, enabling developers to build agricultural solutions using AI and cloud computing tools.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers for the Gemini API to help developers balance costs and latency requirements effectively.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids now offers free AI-powered video creation, editing, and sharing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 for high-quality video generation without cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to protect the country's forests from deforestation and environmental threats.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most advanced open models designed for complex reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence and capabilities.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google releases Gemini 3.1 Flash Live, a new voice model with improved precision and lower latency for more natural and fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches how AI systems can manipulate people in finance and healthcare, developing safety measures to prevent harmful outcomes.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and higher per-token prices, claiming token-efficiency gains. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family with mixture of experts architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launched DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance video game graphics in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene details and lighting. The technology blends traditional 3D rendering with AI for photorealistic improvements.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub code review pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: KiloClaw targets shadow AI with autonomous agent governance
articles.summary: KiloClaw is an enterprise platform designed to govern autonomous agents and manage shadow AI deployments. It addresses the BYOAI trend where employees bypass official procurement to deploy agents, exposing proprietary data to unregulated environments.

articles.title: 5 best practices to secure AI systems
articles.summary: AI systems require multi-layered security strategies including data protection, access control, and monitoring. Five foundational practices address emerging risks from AI's expanding capabilities and attack surfaces.

articles.title: China’s Five-Year Plan details the targets for AI deployment
articles.summary: China's 15th Five-Year Plan through 2030 prioritizes AI alongside quantum computing and biotechnology, emphasizing high-performance chip development, software infrastructure, and advanced model research. Communications technologies including 5G+ and 6G will support AI deployment.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, advancing debugging capabilities for collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture designed to improve autonomous robot navigation in complex indoor environments by addressing localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by effectively balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, overcoming historical limitations in AI models.

articles.title: Multi-level AI prompt engineering: A new tool for scientific discovery
articles.summary: Multi-level prompt engineering techniques enabled an LLM to function as a theoretical collaborator, using parabolic extrapolation to develop a testable multiverse model for scientific discovery.

articles.title: Netflix AI Team Just Open-Sourced VOID: an AI Model That Erases Objects From Videos — Physics and All
articles.summary: Netflix released VOID, an AI model that removes objects from videos while automatically handling physics interactions like falling objects, solving a major video editing challenge that previously required weeks of manual VFX work.

articles.title: How to Build Production-Ready Agentic Systems with Z.AI GLM-5 Using Thinking Mode, Tool Calling, Streaming, and Multi-Turn Workflows
articles.summary: This tutorial demonstrates building production-ready agentic systems using Z.AI's GLM-5 model, covering thinking mode, tool calling, streaming, and multi-turn workflows for real-world applications.

articles.title: Google DeepMind’s Research Lets an LLM Rewrite Its Own Game Theory Algorithms — And It Outperformed the Experts
articles.summary: Google DeepMind's AlphaEvolve uses LLMs to automatically design game theory algorithms for multi-agent reinforcement learning, outperforming manually-created baselines in imperfect-information games like poker.

articles.title: I let a smart planter maintain itself while I was away for 2 months - here's the result
articles.summary: A smart planter successfully maintained a plant autonomously for two months without human intervention. The LeafyPod device can help even inexperienced plant owners keep their plants alive.

articles.title: How to clear your Android phone cache - and why it greatly improves performance
articles.summary: Clearing your Android phone cache is a simple tweak that instantly boosts speed and responsiveness on almost any device by improving performance.

articles.title: Don't plug these 7 common household gadgets into an extension cord - according to an electrician
articles.summary: Seven common household gadgets shouldn't be plugged into extension cords as they pose safety risks, especially during cold weather. Larger appliances require direct wall outlets to prevent electrical hazards.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are evolving from backend dashboards to more visible applications. Key trends emerging for 2026 include increased integration and accessibility of ML technologies.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: Agentic AI systems use state-managed interruptions to halt agent execution pipelines intentionally, implementing human-in-the-loop approval gates for better control and safety.

articles.title: From Prompt to Prediction: Understanding Prefill, Decode, and the KV Cache in LLMs
articles.summary: Article explains LLM inference phases: prefill (processing prompts), decode (generating predictions), and KV cache optimization for efficient decoding.

articles.title: Building a Python Workflow That Catches Bugs Before Production
articles.summary: Modern tooling can identify software defects earlier in the development lifecycle, preventing bugs from reaching production environments.

articles.title: Building Robust Credit Scoring Models with Python
articles.summary: A practical guide on building robust credit scoring models in Python, focusing on measuring variable relationships for effective feature selection in credit risk assessment.

articles.title: DenseNet Paper Walkthrough: All Connected
articles.summary: DenseNet addresses the vanishing gradient problem in deep neural networks by enabling better weight updates during training. The architecture's dense connections improve gradient flow and model performance in very deep networks.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights breakthroughs in physical AI and robots transforming industries. Advancements in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models enable faster real-world deployment.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4, a small, efficient open model family for local agentic AI across devices from RTX PCs to data centers, enabling on-device processing with real-time context.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield 3D strategy game, expanding cloud gaming offerings.

articles.title: Simulate realistic users to evaluate multi-turn AI agents in Strands Evals
articles.summary: Strands Evals framework enables systematic evaluation of multi-turn AI agents by simulating realistic user interactions, extending beyond single-turn assessments with evaluators for helpfulness, faithfulness, and tool usage.

articles.title: Scaling seismic foundation models on AWS: Distributed training with Amazon SageMaker HyperPod and expanding context windows
articles.summary: TGS partnered with AWS to optimize seismic foundation models using Amazon SageMaker HyperPod, achieving near-linear scaling for distributed training and expanding context windows. This infrastructure modernization reduced training time from 6 months to 5 days.

articles.title: Control which domains your AI agents can access
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore enables secure AI agent web access by allowing domain filtering through AWS Network Firewall within VPC, preventing unauthorized access and data exfiltration while protecting against malware.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: NVIDIA accelerates vision AI pipelines using batch mode VC-6 and Nsight tools to improve throughput across decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Google launches Gemma 4 multimodal and multilingual models designed to scale from data centers to edge devices. The models span deployments across NVIDIA Blackwell and Jetson platforms.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: AI inference latency has been reduced to single-digit microseconds for algorithmic trading, enabling faster response times in high-speed electronic markets compared to traditional specialized hardware solutions.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines generational differences in consumer attitudes toward social media ads across various platforms, revealing how age groups perceive and interact with advertising content differently.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background successfully developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough advancements in cutting-edge tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to understand LLM behavior at scale through feature attribution and interpretability analysis, aiming to make AI decision-making more transparent and trustworthy.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents an information-driven approach to designing imaging systems that uses noisy measurements and noise models to optimize how well measurements distinguish objects.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm using divide-and-conquer approach instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks without TD learning's limitations.
